expectations.text: Produces a structured conformance review report with a clear overall verdict
expectations.evidence: Titled 'Conformance Report' with 'Verdict: Non-conformant'; follows the SKILL.md template (Deviations / Conformant / Not applicable / Summary).

expectations.text: Identifies the relevant component(s): Backend HTTP Routes and the service/database layer
expectations.evidence: Lists 'Component(s) reviewed': Backend HTTP Routes, Backend Services, Database Modules & Models, Celery & Background Workers.

expectations.text: Flags that business logic lives in the route handler, violating the thin-edges standard (Principle 2)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 2 [P2] Thin edges, thick core: format validation, quota computation, record creation, and generation are all inline in the handler.

expectations.text: Flags that the route reaches directly into the database and models instead of delegating to a service (Principle 1)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 1 [P1]: the route imports get_session and the Export model, runs select() queries, and reaches past the service layer into database/ and models/.

expectations.text: Flags the missing typed Pydantic request and response schemas (Principle 7)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 3 [P7]: bare str args bind as query params and responses are untyped dicts; recommends Pydantic request/response models.

expectations.text: Flags that the slow export generation runs inline in the request path instead of on a queue (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 7 [P19]: write_export_file runs synchronously in the handler; recommends enqueueing a Celery task and returning 202.

expectations.text: Flags that creating an export is not idempotent, so a retried POST creates duplicate records (Principle 15)
expectations.evidence: Deviation 8 [P15] and 11: no idempotency key; a retried request creates a second export row; recommends create-or-return-existing semantics.

expectations.text: Gives a concrete, specific remediation for each flagged deviation and assigns a severity to it
expectations.evidence: Every deviation entry has an explicit Severity (blocking/should-fix/minor) and a concrete Remediation.

expectations.text: Cites the appsmith-v2 principle numbers behind each finding rather than only generic commentary
expectations.evidence: Every finding is tagged with its principle number ([P1], [P2], [P7], [P19], [P15], plus P28, P20, P9, P5, P17, P8, P12).
